burial.plot_notes: Grove Hill Cemetery, Oil City Venango County PA — with wife Gertrude.

career.notes: Forty years in the shipping department of Pennzoil Refinery at Rouseville, retiring in 1986. Hobbies: lawn care + landscape, woodworking, H-O scale model railroads.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Oil City, forty years at Pennzoil Refinery
narrative_blocks.body: William Winger was born February 6, 1922 in Oil City Pennsylvania — the same Venango County oil region where his wife's great-grandmother Florence Hannah Ellis had been born at Pithole fifty-eight years earlier. He spent forty years at Pennzoil Refinery's shipping department in Rouseville, retiring in 1986. The Pennsylvania oil industry that had briefly turned Pithole into a fifteen-thousand-person boomtown in 1865 became the family livelihood three generations later.

narrative_blocks.header: Sixty year marriage, hobbyist woodworker and railroader
narrative_blocks.body: William married Gertrude Virginia Young on December 7, 1951 — the tenth anniversary of Pearl Harbor. They celebrated their sixtieth anniversary in 2011 and he died the following year on June 29, 2012 at ninety. He enjoyed lawn care, woodworking, and H-O scale model railroads in retirement.

bio.photo_notes: Portrait of Gertrude Virginia Young Winger (1926-2014).

burial.plot_notes: Grove Hill Cemetery, Oil City Venango County PA — with husband William Burton Winger.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: William Burton Winger (I00680, 1922-2012). Five children per Gertrude's 2014 obit: Michael Winger (Lakewood CO), Luann Valdesalice (Franklin PA), Laura Winger (Spring Lake NC), Gerald Winger (Gaston SC), Amy Liddle (Rochester NY). Seven grandchildren also named in obit.

career.notes: Employed many years as a nurse, retiring from Franklin Hospital. Multiple Methodist church memberships across Venango County and Chautauqua County NY.

narrative_blocks.header: Same Valentine's Day birthday as uncle Ransom Unger
narrative_blocks.body: Gertrude was born February 14, 1926 in Meadville Pennsylvania — sharing a Valentine's Day birthday with her maternal uncle Ransom Andrew Unger, born exactly thirty-eight years earlier on February 14, 1888. Both members of the same extended Crawford County Ellis-Unger family separated by a generation. Her mother Bertha would have known the family date.

narrative_blocks.header: Wedding date was tenth anniversary of Pearl Harbor
narrative_blocks.body: Gertrude married William Burton Winger on December 7, 1951 at Cochranton Pennsylvania — the ten-year anniversary of the Pearl Harbor attack. She was twenty-five, he was twenty-nine. They celebrated their sixtieth wedding anniversary in 2011, the year before his death.

narrative_blocks.header: Last surviving among eleven predeceased siblings
narrative_blocks.body: When Gertrude died in January 2014, she had outlived eleven documented half-siblings — nine Young, Stanley, and Free half-siblings from her mother's second marriage, plus Chester, Irvin, and Wilbur Lybarger from her mother's first marriage. Her own marriage to William Winger lasted sixty years until his June 2012 death, and she followed him eighteen months later.

burialCemetery.name: Grove Hill Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Grove Hill Cemetery, Oil City Venango County PA. Burial site of Gertrude Virginia Young Winger (HD4288, gen 10 Hooker descendant) and her husband William Burton Winger — both buried in Venango County's Oil City, the same county where Florence Hannah Ellis was born at Pithole City in 1864.
